facts.text: Sun City Center was founded by Del Webb Corp as Florida's first planned retirement community, inspired by Sun City Arizona.
The project, originally called "Del Webb's Sun City," opened December 30, 1961, launching a new model for affordable retirement living.
In early 1961, Del Webb bought about 12,000 acres of former cattle ranch and tomato farmland in Hillsborough County to build the community.
A Florida Historical Marker was dedicated at the community's entrance in 2023, honoring Sun City Center as the state's first planned retirement community.
The community library, founded in 1963 by Erma Krauch with an initial donation of 5,000 books, remains a private resource for members.
Sun City Center lies south of Tampa and north of Sarasota along I‑75, placing it in southern Hillsborough County.
